caption:
The fastest free way for a local business to climb Google's local results is also the one most owners ignore. Reviews. Here's the 4-step system we set up for every client ↓

1. Ask in person, at the peak moment. The job is done, the customer is happy. That's the window. An email a week later gets ignored. If you're owner-operated, you're the one finishing the job, so you're the one who can ask.

2. Hand them the direct link. Google gives every profile a short review link. "Look us up on Google" adds friction, and friction is where reviews die. Save the link in your phone and text it on the spot.

3. Reply to every review, good and bad. Google's local ranking reads owner replies as a sign of a real, active business. A reply to a bad review isn't for the upset customer. It's for the next 100 people who read it.

4. Never buy reviews. Google's filter catches fake patterns and the penalty can be a full profile suspension. A slow, real review profile beats a fast, fake one every time.

That's the whole system. No budget needed, just a habit.

What's your business sitting at for Google reviews right now?
